geographic_restrictions: Working hours must meaningfully overlap with US or EU timezones
experience_requirements: Experience shipping SaaS or software products to production
Experience owning user-facing product work from ambiguous problem to shipped feature
Experience with technical customer calls and translating customer needs into product changes
Hands-on experience using LLMs daily and shipping at least one feature where value depends on LLM output quality
required_skills: Judging or evaluation of LLM outputs
Remote collaboration across US or EU timezones
soft_skills: collaboration with developers and security researchers
key_responsibilities: Talk directly with protocol teams and turn product needs into shipped product or UI changes
Own frontend and product surfaces in Next.js and TypeScript from UX flow to working implementation
Improve prompting, judging, deduplication, and false-positive reduction for an AI security product
Build and maintain benchmarks and evaluations for LLM-based security outputs
Make product calls in ambiguous areas without waiting for fully scoped tickets
Work closely with developers, security researchers, auditors, and customers
Stay current with AI and LLM workflows and apply them to product development
success_outcomes: Protocol customers can understand and act on security findings more easily
Sherlock AI produces clearer findings with fewer false positives and less duplicate noise
Customer conversations turn into fast, useful product improvements
Security workflows connect development, audit, remediation, and reporting
AI product quality improves through better prompts, judging, benchmarks, and evaluation loops
must_have_checklist: Production SaaS or product engineering experience
Modern frontend ownership with Next.js and TypeScript
Ability to turn UX flows and states into working product UI
Shipped feature whose value depends on LLM output quality such as prompting, judging, retrieval, or evals
Evidence of turning customer conversations into product changes
High-agency execution in underdefined product areas
Working-hours overlap with US or EU timezones
red_flags: Pure backend, prompt, or UI specialist without product ownership
No genuine interest in UI or UX implementation
No comfort with technical customer calls
Needs fully scoped tickets before starting work
